title: Trump: 'The USA Needs Nothing From NATO' — Warns Alliance Will 'Remember' Iran Refusal
detail: President Trump posted on Truth Social at approximately 6:16 a.m. ET: 'the USA needs nothing from NATO,' adding 'never forget' — framing the moment as a direct warning to all alliance members that their Iran refusal would have lasting consequences. Bloomberg reported Trump 'assails NATO' and says the US will "'remember' their response on Iran." The statement marked an escalation beyond Trump's previous 'cowards' broadside, explicitly casting the alliance as a net burden rather than a US strategic asset. European capitals immediately registered alarm, with senior officials privately noting Trump appeared to be moving from conditional commitment to outright rejection of the alliance framework.

title: Rutte Launches NATO Annual Report, Claims Alliance 'Safer Under Trump' as All 32 Members Hit 2% for First Time
detail: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte held a press conference to launch the NATO Annual Report 2025, declaring that NATO is 'safer under Trump' and crediting Trump's pressure tactics with forcing all 32 alliance members to meet the 2% GDP defense spending threshold for the first time in NATO's history. Rutte framed this as vindication of his pro-Trump diplomatic strategy. The annual report confirmed a 19.6% surge in allied military expenditure. Rutte acknowledged European frustration with his Iran stance but said keeping the US inside NATO was worth the political cost. The 'all 32 at 2%' milestone was widely covered as a historic first — though critics noted it arrived just as Trump was publicly questioning whether the alliance held any value for the United States.
